text: The video shows a contracted gallbladder encased in a layer of surrounding fat, as observed during the robotic cholecystectomy procedure.
The surgeon switches to indocyanine green imaging, but the cystic duct does not illuminate as expected in cases of acute cholecystitis.

The surgeon uses four robotic arms to perform dissection, aiming to achieve the critical view of safety. Arm one holds a ProGrasp, arm two operates a Maryland Dissector, arm three controls the camera, and arm four utilizes a hook cautery.
The surgeon dissects the cystic artery, ensuring adequate space for placing three clips. The margin of the liver is visible, and the critical view of safety has been obtained.
The surgeon places clips on the cystic artery, with occasional bleeding observed at the site, which is managed straightforwardly.
The surgeon uses cautery to address a small bleeding site, which stops immediately upon application.
The surgeon proceeds to dissect down to the cystic plate during the cholecystectomy, using standard techniques.
